content: Hepatitis B virus (HBV) is a partially double-stranded DNA hepadnavirus causing acute and chronic hepatitis. Transmission: percutaneous, sexual, perinatal (high chronicity risk if infected at birth). Natural history: incubation 60-150 days; acute infection resolves in 95% of immunocompetent adults (HBsAg clearance, anti-HBs development); 5% develop chronic infection (HBsAg >6 months). Chronic phases: immune tolerant (HBeAg+, high DNA, normal ALT), immune active (HBeAg+/anti-HBe+, elevated ALT, liver inflammation), inactive carrier (HBeAg-, anti-HBe+, low DNA, normal ALT), HBeAg-negative chronic hepatitis (mutant virus, fluctuating DNA/ALT). Complications: cirrhosis (15-40%), hepatocellular carcinoma (annual risk 2-8% in cirrhosis). Vaccination highly effective (95% seroprotection); universal infant vaccination implemented globally.
